title: linaria supina subsp. propinqua
intro: Linaria supina subsp. propinqua, also known as the lesser toadflax or small toadflax, is a flowering plant species in the figwort family, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. Consuming Linaria supina subsp. propinqua has been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
